Title: Molecular basis of very-long-chain fatty acid elongation by the CER6-GL2 enzyme complex in plant wax biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Very-long-chain fatty acids (VLCFAs; >C28) for cuticular wax biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant cuticular wax formation; hydrophobic barrier associated with drought tolerance and pest resistance
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cuticular wax biosynthesis / very-long-chain fatty acid elongation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ER fatty acid elongase condensation step for >C28 acyl-chain elongation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: condensation step; chain-length extension control step for >C28 VLCFA elongation when partnered with GL2
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for >C28 elongation in complex with GL2
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms ketoacyl-CoA intermediates leading to VLCFA products
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: KCS family; FAE/β-ketoacyl-ACP synthase III-like catalytic domain; thiolase fold
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses very-long-chain acyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to produce elongated β-ketoacyl-CoA, extending the acyl chain by two carbons
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In complex with ZmGL2, enables sequential elongation of VLCFA substrates beyond C28, with preference for 30:0-CoA to form 32:0-ketoacyl-CoA; alone shows no detectable activity in this assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression in insect cells; LC-MS-based in vitro enzyme assay; cryo-EM structure; site-directed mutagenesis; pull-down assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmCER6 is the catalytic KCS subunit of the maize CER6-GL2 elongase complex. 2. ZmCER6 requires ZmGL2 for detectable condensation activity in vitro and preferentially elongates 30:0-CoA to 32:0-ketoacyl-CoA in the complex. 3. Cryo-EM revealed a membrane-anchored ZmCER6 homodimer with substrate tunnel and a Cys-His-Asn catalytic triad. 4. Mutations in catalytic/substrate-binding residues such as Cys222, His389, and Asn422 abolished activity, confirming catalytic function.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays with malonyl-CoA and 26:0/28:0/30:0-CoA substrates
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for engineering wax composition and stress-resilient crops through modulation of VLCFA elongation and cuticular wax output
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Uses a distinct Cys-His-Asn catalytic triad rather than the histidine-dependent catalysis of mammalian elongases; ZmCER6 alone lacks detectable condensation activity in this study, unlike AtCER6 which retains intrinsic 26:0-CoA elongation activity.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Very-long-chain fatty acids (VLCFAs) for cuticular wax biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant cuticular wax formation; hydrophobic barrier
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cuticular wax biosynthesis / very-long-chain fatty acid elongation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ER fatty acid elongase condensation step
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step, but not established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms ketoacyl-CoA intermediates leading to VLCFAs
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: KCS family; FAE/β-ketoacyl-ACP synthase III-like catalytic domain
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses VLC acyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form elongated β-ketoacyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtCER6-AtCER2 converts 28:0-CoA to 30:0-ketoacyl-CoA, and AtCER6 alone retains intrinsic 26:0-CoA elongation activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LC-MS-based in vitro enzyme assay of heterologously expressed proteins
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCER6 serves as the Arabidopsis catalytic comparison enzyme for CER6-GL2/CER2 complexes. 2. In complex with AtCER2 it elongates 28:0-CoA to 30:0-ketoacyl-CoA. 3. Unlike ZmCER6, AtCER6 alone shows intrinsic 26:0-CoA elongation activity, highlighting species-divergent substrate preference.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays with malonyl-CoA and 26:0/28:0-CoA substrates
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful comparative enzyme for tuning chain-length specificity in wax engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included primarily as an evolutionary and biochemical comparator to maize CER6.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: auxiliary BAHD-family modulator / noncatalytic elongase regulator
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase-like protein with HxxxD motif
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Very-long-chain fatty acids (VLCFAs; >C28) for cuticular wax biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant cuticular wax formation; hydrophobic barrier associated with drought tolerance and pest resistance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Chain-length extension and substrate specificity control during VLCFA elongation in wax biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential modulator that confers/remodels ZmCER6 activity to enable elongation beyond C28
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Binding of ZmGL2 to ZmCER6 is required for detectable elongation of 30:0-CoA to 32:0-ketoacyl-CoA and for accommodation of >C28 acyl chains
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Allosteric/noncatalytic modulation via heterodimer formation and substrate tunnel remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a 2:2 ZmCER6-ZmGL2 heterotetramer with a ZmCER6 homodimer and two ZmGL2 molecules
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosolic side of endoplasmic reticulum-associated complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression and LC-MS in vitro enzymatic assay; cryo-EM structure; pull-down assay; site-directed mutagenesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmGL2 forms a stable complex with ZmCER6 and is required for complex-dependent VLCFA elongation activity. 2. Structural analysis shows ZmGL2 remodels the ZmCER6 substrate tunnel into a continuous hydrophobic channel accommodating >C28 acyl chains. 3. Mutations at the ZmCER6-ZmGL2 interface disrupt complex formation and abolish activity. 4. The conserved HxxxD motif is largely dispensable for activity and dimerization, indicating ZmGL2 functions noncatalytically rather than as a classical BAHD acyltransferase.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays with malonyl-CoA and VLC acyl-CoA substrates
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering wax composition by modifying CER6-GL2 interface or isoform pairing to alter VLCFA chain-length output and improve drought/pest resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although it contains a conserved HxxxD motif typical of BAHD enzymes, this motif is spatially separated from the CER6 active site and is not required for core modulatory function except that G172A partially impairs stability/activity.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: auxiliary BAHD-family modulator / noncatalytic elongase regulator
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase-like protein with HxxxD motif
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Very-long-chain fatty acids (VLCFAs) for cuticular wax biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant cuticular wax formation; hydrophobic barrier
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Substrate specificity control during VLCFA elongation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Reprograms AtCER6 substrate specificity in the CER6-CER2 complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: With AtCER6, supports conversion of 28:0-CoA to 30:0-ketoacyl-CoA; used as comparator showing species-divergent CER2/GL2-dependent substrate preference
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Likely noncatalytic modulation via complex formation with AtCER6
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LC-MS-based in vitro enzyme assay of heterologously expressed AtCER6-AtCER2 complex
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCER2 acts with AtCER6 in the Arabidopsis elongase complex. 2. The AtCER6-AtCER2 complex elongates 28:0-CoA to 30:0-ketoacyl-CoA. 3. Comparison with maize supports the idea that CER2/GL2 homologs act as evolutionary switches that reprogram CER6 substrate specificity.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays with malonyl-CoA and VLC acyl-CoA substrates
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Comparator useful for isoform pairing strategies in wax metabolic engineering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included mainly as comparative evidence rather than the primary structural subject of the paper.
